title: The Dental Partnership Decision: What 100+ Practice Owners Learned After Saying Yes
titleOptions: The Dental Partnership Decision: What 100+ Practice Owners Learned After Saying Yes
7 Out of 10 Dentists Regret Selling to a DSO -- Here Is How to Be in the Other 30%
The 7-Question Framework That Predicts Whether You Will Be Happy or Miserable After Joining a DSO

registrationCopy.headline: The Dental Partnership Decision: What 100+ Practice Owners Learned After Saying Yes
registrationCopy.subheadline: A live masterclass on evaluating DSO partnerships -- taught by a practicing dentist who has sat across the table from over 100 practice owners making this decision.
bulletPoints: The 3 partnership models that actually exist in dental consolidation -- and the structural differences that determine your experience
A 7-question diagnostic framework that predicts whether you will end up in the happy 30% or the miserable 70% -- works against any DSO, including ours
The economics nobody explains: EBITDA multiples, equity mechanics, second-bite math, and why the same 'multiple' can mean wildly different things
Why the PE sponsor behind a DSO matters more than the deal terms themselves -- and the one question your financial advisor should be asking
A special offer for everyone who stays until the end: a 1-on-1 peer conversation with a current partner dentist (not a sales call -- a peer conversation)
registrationCopy.speakerBio: Dr. Mitch Ellingson is a practicing dentist with 20+ years of experience, CCO and co-founder of Gen4 Dental Partners (now SGA Dental Partners). He has personally sat across the table from 100+ dentists evaluating the partnership decision. Lamonte Jensen is CEO of SGA Dental Partners, a former Marine Corps infantry officer and McKinsey consultant. Together they built a dental partnership platform backed by the Thurston Group (38 years, 8 platforms, $4B+ returned to partners).
registrationCopy.socialProof: Join 100+ practice owners who have used this framework to make better partnership decisions

reminderEmails.subject: Tomorrow: The Partnership Decision Framework (Live with Dr. Ellingson)
reminderEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

Quick reminder: tomorrow's live session starts at [Time] [Timezone].

The Dental Partnership Decision: What 100+ Practice Owners Learned After Saying Yes

Here is what we are covering:

- The 3 partnership models and why most dentists pick the wrong one
- A 7-question framework that predicts happy vs. miserable outcomes
- The economics nobody explains (multiples, equity, second-bite math)
- A special offer for everyone who stays until the end

Your join link: [LINK]

One thing to prepare: think about what 'Monday morning' looks like for you right now. How many hours are you spending on non-clinical work? What is the one thing about practice ownership that drains you most?

Dr. Ellingson is going to address those specific scenarios during the session.

See you tomorrow.

[Your Name]
SGA Dental Partners

reminderEmails.subject: Starting in 60 minutes -- your join link is inside
reminderEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

We go live in 60 minutes.

The Dental Partnership Decision: What 100+ Practice Owners Learned After Saying Yes

Join link: [LINK]

Dr. Ellingson and Lamonte Jensen are both on camera. Bring your questions -- the Q&A at the end is where the real conversations happen.

See you shortly.

reminderEmails.subject: We are LIVE -- join now
reminderEmails.body:
Dr. [Name],

We are live right now.

Join here: [LINK]

Dr. Ellingson is starting with the number that changed everything for us: 7 out of 10.

Do not miss the opening.

opening.script:
MITCH:
"Seven out of ten.

That is the number that changed everything for us. According to the Dentist Advisors podcast -- and if you listen to it, you know they have no reason to lie -- seven out of ten dentists who sell to a DSO end up frustrated, annoyed, or outright regretful. Do I hear 'best decision I ever made'? Their words: 'It is pretty rare.'

Let me be direct with you. My name is Dr. Mitch Ellingson. I am a practicing dentist. I have owned multiple practices. I have personally lived the transition from private practice to working inside a group. And I am going to tell you something that most people on webinars will not tell you:

My agenda today is to educate you so thoroughly that if you decide to explore a partnership with our organization -- SGA Dental Partners -- it is because you made an informed decision, not because I pitched you.

But here is my other agenda: if I do NOT deliver genuine value in the next 60 minutes -- if you do not walk away with a framework you can use tomorrow, regardless of whether you ever speak to us again -- I absolutely insist you do not give us another second of your time.

Does that sound fair? If so, type 'fair' in the chat."

[Pause for chat responses. Acknowledge: 'I see the chat lighting up. Good. Hold me to it.']

MITCH:
"Before we dive in, let me tell you who you are spending the next hour with. I am a practicing dentist. I still see patients every week. I have owned multiple practices in Phoenix for over 20 years. I founded Spear Practice Solutions. I co-founded Gen4 Dental Partners, which grew from 5 group practices to 90 in 20 months -- now part of SGA Dental Partners."

LAMONTE:
"I'm Lamonte Jensen. Before dental, I was a Marine Corps infantry officer -- multiple combat tours in Iraq. Then I earned my MBA at the University of Chicago, consulted at McKinsey, and spent years as VP at Spear Education. SGA is backed by the Thurston Group -- 38 years, 8 platforms, $4 billion returned to partners. One more name: Dr. Michael Pikos. The Pikos Institute is part of this platform."

MITCH:
"Here is what we are covering: the three partnership models, a seven-question diagnostic framework, and the economics that nobody explains. For everyone who stays until the end, I am going to share something we have never offered publicly before. Type 'committed' in the chat if you are staying with us."
opening.notes: Opening establishes the 7-out-of-10 stat, sets the educational frame, and commits the audience with two chat prompts ('fair' and 'committed'). Both speakers appear on camera.

teaching.insight: The 3 Partnership Models That Actually Exist
teaching.script:
MITCH:
"Everything I am going to share today comes down to one insight: The partnership model matters less than you think. The 18-month reality matters more than you know.

Model 1: The Corporate Acquirer (Gen2 DSO). They buy your practice outright. You sign an employment agreement. Your name may or may not survive. Clinical decisions are increasingly influenced by corporate protocols. Your financial upside ends at closing. This is Heartland, Aspen, DCA.

Model 2: The Management Company. They do not buy your practice. You pay them 15-25% of collections for operational support. No platform equity. No second bite. You are paying for a service, not joining a partnership.

Model 3: The Equity Partnership (Gen4/IDSO). You sell a portion (55-70%), receive meaningful liquidity, and retain 30-45% as equity in the combined platform. Clinical autonomy is contractual. Your name stays. Your team stays.

The critical distinction: not all equity partnerships are the same. Over 1,000 organizations now call themselves IDSOs. Fewer than 100 are considered qualified."

LAMONTE:
"Here is why 'clinical autonomy' is the most meaningless phrase in dental consolidation. Every DSO claims it. But there is no standard definition. The follow-up question that separates real from theater: 'Is your clinical independence clause written into the partnership agreement, and what happens if it is violated?'

If they cannot answer with specifics -- contract language, dispute resolution, real enforcement -- then what they are offering is not clinical autonomy. It is a marketing bullet point."

MITCH:
"The question is not 'does this DSO SAY clinical autonomy?' The question is 'what does Monday morning LOOK like 18 months after you sign?' Write that question down. It is the most important question in this entire conversation."
teaching.slideNotes: Slide 1: 'The Partnership Model Matters Less Than You Think. The 18-Month Reality Matters More.' Slide 2-4: Each model with icons. Slide 5: 'Why Clinical Autonomy Is the Most Meaningless Phrase in Dental Consolidation'

teaching.insight: The 7 Questions That Predict Whether You'll Be in the Happy 30% or the Miserable 70%
teaching.script:
MITCH:
"This is the section I am most excited to share. We developed this framework internally and we have never taught it publicly. Here are the seven questions.

Question 1: Who makes clinical decisions after the deal closes -- and is it in writing? If the clinical independence clause is not in the contract, it does not exist.

Question 2: What does my practice name and brand look like in Year 3? Ask them to show you three practices that joined five years ago. What are their names today?

Question 3: What is the PE sponsor's track record?"

LAMONTE:
"How many times has this PE firm built a dental platform? How long have they been at it? What happened to the partners in their previous platforms? Can I talk to one of them? If they have done this once, they are experimenting with your career."

MITCH:
"Question 4: What does the equity model look like -- the term sheet version, not the pitch deck version? Ask for base case, bull case, and bear case.

Question 5: What operational support arrives in the first 90 days? Walk me through week by week what changes. Who handles payroll migration? Benefits enrollment? Who is my point of contact when something breaks at 7 AM?

Question 6: What is the staff retention rate across the network? Most DSOs do not track this or will not share it. If they cannot give you the number, that IS the answer.

Question 7: Can I speak directly with 3+ current partners of MY choosing? Not partners they select. Partners you select. Pick three at random. Call them.

Type 'got it' in the chat if you are going to use this framework."

"Good. Now here is the harder truth. The framework tells you who to trust. It does not tell you whether partnership is right for you in the first place. That is a financial question."
teaching.slideNotes: Each question gets its own slide with bold text. Final slide: all 7 questions on one page as a summary framework.

teaching.script:
LAMONTE:
"Let me start with multiples. You will hear 5x, 7x, 10x. But how is EBITDA calculated? Trailing twelve months or adjusted with add-backs? The same practice can have EBITDA of $600K or $900K depending on methodology. A 7x deal on $600K is $4.2M. On $900K it is $6.3M. That is a $2.1 million difference.

Second: what is the actual cash at close? A 7x deal does not mean 7x in cash. 65% cash on a $5M deal is $3.25M, not $5M.

Third: earnouts. 15-20% tied to revenue retention over 2-3 years. If your hygienist leaves, your revenue dips, you may not hit the threshold.

Now let me explain the concept that makes equity partnerships fundamentally different: the second bite. You receive two financial events: cash at closing (first bite) and equity appreciation when the platform transacts (second bite). Industry analysts: 'In many cases, the retained minority equity position will be far more valuable in the future.'

But the second bite only works if the PE sponsor has a track record.

One question your financial advisor should ask: 'What is the PE sponsor's historical return to limited partners across prior healthcare platforms, and what was the median hold period?'

Write that question down. Send it to your advisor tonight."

MITCH:
"Let me make this personal. Dr. Matt Cavendish. 20 years in practice. After partnering, he was mentored, learned all-on-four, revenue up 16.5%, staff retention 100%. But here is the number Matt talks about most: 'It is fun.' Fun. After 20 years. No spreadsheet captures that. But it is the thing that separates the happy 30% from the miserable 70%."
teaching.slideNotes: EBITDA multiple ranges graphic. The Second Bite visual showing two financial events. 'What Your Financial Advisor Should Be Asking' slide.

transition.script:
MITCH:
"Let me take a breath here.

You now know the three partnership models and the structural differences between them. You have a seven-question framework that works against any DSO in the country. You understand the economics at a level that 95% of practice owners never reach.

That is what we promised. And I meant what I said: if this is all you take away and you never contact us again, you are still better equipped.

But I would be doing you a disservice if I did not share what we have built. Because everything I just taught you was developed from the inside. From building SGA Dental Partners. From making mistakes and fixing them. From sitting across the table from over 100 dentists.

So let me take three minutes and show you what this looks like when someone actually builds a partnership platform around these principles."

offer.script:
MITCH:
"Rather than giving you a pitch deck, I am going to walk you through SGA using the same seven questions.

Q1: Who makes clinical decisions? You do. In writing. The Clinical Independence Clause specifies treatment plans, materials, lab, schedule remain with the partner. I am the CCO -- a practicing dentist.

Q2: What does my name look like in Year 3? Dental Partners of SW Georgia is still Dental Partners of SW Georgia. Periodontal Associates of Memphis is still Periodontal Associates of Memphis. We do not rebrand. Period."

LAMONTE:
"Q3: PE track record? The Thurston Group. 38 years. 8 platforms. $4B+ returned. Smile Doctors, US Endodontics Partners, US Oral Surgery Management, ARC Health.

Q4: Equity model? JV equity partnership. 60-70% upfront. 30-40% retained. We show base, bull, and bear. $350M credit facility for growth."

MITCH:
"Q5: First 90 days? Billing, payroll, HR, compliance, procurement, marketing, recruiting transition within 60-90 days. Spencer Pierce: 'There is an abundance of relief.'

Q6: Staff retention? 85% across the network. Published. No competitor publishes theirs.

Q7: Can I talk to partners? All of them. Dr. Godat. Dr. Cavendish. Dr. Olson. Phone numbers, not testimonials.

Three verticals: Community Dentistry, Collaborative General and Specialty, Advanced Implantology through MODIS. The Pikos Institute. The 4Front Symposium. The Individual Growth Engine. No other DSO has this."

objectionHandling.response: I LIVED this fear. Here is what I discovered: in the right model, you do not lose your voice. You amplify it. We do not tell partners how to practice. We ask them to lead more. And it is in the contract.

objectionHandling.objection: My patients and staff will freak out
objectionHandling.response: Your name stays. Your team stays. 85% staff retention. Benefits upgrade: Cigna, 401(k), PTO. Your office manager goes from 15 hats to two.

objectionHandling.objection: The equity model is too opaque
objectionHandling.response: We show base, bull, and bear. We walk your advisor through the term sheet. Thurston principals take your call. $4B returned is history, not projection.

objectionHandling.objection: PE will flip us in 3-5 years
objectionHandling.response: Thurston has been in business 38 years. Not 3-5. 38. Track record across 8 platforms is verifiable. Partners from prior platforms will take your call.

objectionHandling.objection: I'm not ready -- timing isn't right
objectionHandling.response: Valuations are at cyclical highs. Staffing crisis is not improving. Every Sunday night you spend doing the schedule is a Sunday night you do not get back. A conversation costs 30 minutes. You can always say no.

close.script:
MITCH:
"Before Q&A, here is the offer I mentioned at the beginning.

If you are seriously evaluating your options, I would like to connect you with a current SGA partner for a 1-on-1 conversation. Not a sales call. A peer conversation with a dentist who has been where you are.

This offer is open for 48 hours -- through [Day, Date] at midnight ET.

For everyone who books within 48 hours, you also receive:
1. The Partnership Evaluation Scorecard (printable 7-question framework with scoring rubric)
2. 'The 5 Deal Terms That Matter Most' cheat sheet from Lamonte
3. A confidential practice profile to match you with a partner in a comparable situation

This is not a commitment. It is a 30-minute peer conversation. The worst outcome is you learn something.

[URL] -- link is in the chat."

attendees.subject: The one question I wish I had asked sooner
attendees.body:
Dr. [Name],

During last night's session, I shared the question your financial advisor should be asking:

'What is the PE sponsor's historical return to limited partners across prior healthcare platforms, and what was the median hold period?'

I want to add context that I did not have time to cover live.

The reason this question matters: a PE firm with a 3-year hold and 2x returns is optimizing for a quick flip. Your equity is a vehicle for their exit strategy. A firm with a 38-year track record and $4B+ returned is building compounding value. Your equity grows alongside the platform.

The Thurston Group has been doing this since 1988. Eight platforms. The partners from those prior platforms -- Smile Doctors, US Endodontics Partners, US Oral Surgery Management -- will take your call and tell you what the equity appreciation looked like.

Send that question to your financial advisor today. Their answer will tell you more about the DSOs you are evaluating than any pitch deck.

Book your partner conversation (36 hours remaining): [LINK]

Best,
Lamonte Jensen
SGA Dental Partners

attendees.subject: What Dr. Cavendish said after Year 1
attendees.body:
Dr. [Name],

I shared Dr. Cavendish's story during the webinar, but I want to give you the full picture.

20 years in practice. Revenue was fine. Team was stable. But in his words: 'Sometimes it is not as fun as it used to be.'

After joining SGA:
- Paired with a clinical mentor through the Individual Growth Engine
- Observed his first all-on-four
- Revenue up 16.5% trailing twelve months
- Staff retention: 100%
- EBITDA beating plan by 2.3 points

His word for the experience: fun.

I would like to connect you with a partner in a comparable situation to yours. Not Matt specifically (unless you want to talk to him -- I can arrange that too). But someone whose practice profile and career stage match yours.

Reply to this email with 'connect me' and I will make the introduction.

Best,
Dr. Mitch Ellingson

attendees.subject: The scorecard that started 40+ conversations this month
attendees.body:
Dr. [Name],

Since the webinar, 40+ practice owners have taken the Partnership Readiness Scorecard. The results have been fascinating.

The most common weakest category: Operational Independence. Most practice owners score highest in Clinical Growth (they are good dentists) and lowest in Operational Independence (they are drowning in admin).

The scorecard takes 5 minutes and gives you a personalized analysis with specific recommendations. It works against any partnership model, not just SGA.

Take it here: [LINK]

If your results suggest we should talk, I am available for a confidential 15-minute conversation. No pitch. Just an honest assessment of whether SGA's model addresses your specific situation.

Best,
[BizDev Rep Name]
SGA Dental Partners

attendees.subject: One last resource -- then I will stop emailing
attendees.body:
Dr. [Name],

This is the last planned email in this sequence. I want to leave you with one resource that several attendees have told me was the most valuable thing from the webinar:

The 7-Question Framework (printable PDF): [LINK]

Print it. Bring it to your next DSO meeting. Apply it to everyone -- including us.

If you ever want to pick the conversation back up, reply to any of my emails. No expiration. No awkwardness.

I wish you and your practice well.

Best,
Dr. Mitch Ellingson
SGA Dental Partners
